::: {.exercise}
Can you change the code to show the relation between living in a less densely populated municipality and voting for the BBB (farmer's party)?
:::


```{webr}
#| setup: true
#| exercise: ex_scatter
library(tidyverse)
votes <- read_csv("data/dutch_elections_2023.csv")
demographics <- read_csv("data/dutch_demographics.csv")
combined <- inner_join(votes, demographics)
```

```{webr}
#| exercise: ex_scatter
combined |>
  filter(party == "PVV") |>
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = v43_nl, y = votes)) +
  geom_point()
```

::: { .hint exercise="ex_scatter"}
::: { .callout-tip title="Hint:"}

You can change the name of the party to `"BBB"` directly, remember to put it in quotes since it's a literal text.
The column you're looking for is `v57_density` -- no quotes needed as it's not a literal text for R, but instead it's a name for a column that R knows about

:::
:::

::: {.solution exercise="ex_scatter"}
::: { .callout-important icon=false title="🎓 Solution:"}

Change the name of the party to `"BBB"` directly, remember to put it in quotes since it's a literal text.
Change the `x` column in the mapping to `v57_density` -- no quotes needed as it's not a literal text for R, but instead it's a name for a column that R knows about

```{webr}
#| exercise: ex_scatter
#| solution: true
combined |>
  filter(party == "BBB") |>
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = v57_density, y = votes)) +
  geom_point()
```

:::
:::

```{webr}
#| exercise: ex_scatter
#| check: true
gradethis::grade_this_code()
```
